items.title: PlayStation PULSE Elite headset and Explore earbuds hit new all-time low from $109 for Days of Play 2026 sale
items.summary:
As part of Sony’s now-live PlayStation Days of Play 2026 sale event, you can now snag the brand’s PULSE Elite wireless headset for $109 shipped from Walmart and Amazon. This particular model typically goes for $150, so you’re looking at a $41 discount on it right now, which works out to about 27% off. This is the lowest price we have tracked for this headset, and it’s now going for $20 less than its previous all-time low. If you’d rather get your hands on a pair of earbuds for gaming, Sony’s PlayStation PULSE Explore earbuds are also discounted right now at $149 shipped, down from $200. This is also the lowest price we have seen these earbuds fetch to date, making this a great time to cash in. You can get both the headset and the earbuds in Midnight Black and White finishes, and they’re all down to their lowest prices right now for the Days of Play sale event. Head below for more details.

more…

While it may have a $1,000 full price tagged on, you can more often find it down between $700 and $600 on average, with sales offering bigger discounts to $550, and more recently $500, which we saw for the first time in April. Now, this $200 markdown ($500 off the MSRP) is back around the bend for another go, giving you until the end of the month to grab one for your own commutes at the all-time lowest price we have tracked. items.summary:
We just spotted a deal at Amazon that drops Samsung’s Odyssey 49-inch curved gaming monitor down to $699.99 shipped. This model typically carries a $1,000 regular price, and today’s deal knocks a hefty $300 off that rate to mark one of the lowest prices we have tracked for it to date. While it dipped lower $680 during last year’s Black Friday sales, this is still a compelling offer that lands it within just $20 of that price. It may not be the newest entry in the Odyssey lineup, but it remains a great option for anyone looking to upgrade their battlestation without emptying their pockets for one of the latest models.
